articles.title: Power outages hit France as record heatwave set to peak
articles.summary: About 68,000 homes in Brittany have been affected, with electricity unlikely to be fully restored until Wednesday night.

articles.title: Congress passes war powers measure for first time, breaking with Trump over Iran
articles.summary: The resolution passed on Tuesday was largely symbolic, but it adds to pressure on the White House to end the conflict once and for all.

articles.title: UN says it will evacuate sailors stranded in Strait of Hormuz, as Rubio warns against tolls
articles.summary: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio warned Iran that no country can charge fees for ships to travel through the strait.

articles.title: UN nuclear chief says inspectors will visit Iran sites as part of war deal
articles.summary: Rafael Grossi says the IAEA is "working on modalities" but an Iranian minister says such access would only be part of a final deal with the US.

articles.title: French woman allegedly held captive by husband for 12 years rescued in Pakistan
articles.summary: Yasmina alleges she was held along with her five children, and they were all cut off from the outside world.

articles.title: Sydney woman wakes from induced coma more than a week after shark attack
articles.summary: Leah Stewart, 34, had one of her arms amputated after she was bitten while swimming at Coogee Beach.

articles.title: Eight sentenced to 450 years in prison over anti-ICE riot where officer was shot
articles.summary: An officer was shot in the neck during the Texas disorder by "Antifa Cell operatives", prosecutors said.
